commit_message: pins.h, tweaked FIFOs, added a test result

user_prompt: are we configuring `mck_io_num` at all anywhere?
agent_reply: No, not in our app code.
- Our real speaker path in [AudioManager.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:819) sets:
so for the NS4168 speaker path we are explicitly not using MCLK / `mck_io_num`.
- Our pin constants only define BCLK, LRCK, DOUT in [pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:9).

- `M5.begin(cfg)` in [all_init.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:100) enables `cfg.internal_spk = true`, so M5Unified may build its own temporary speaker config from its board profile. But immediately after begin we call:
so our audio manager later owns I2S directly.

Inside `.pio/libdeps`, M5Unified does assign `pin_mck` for some board profiles, and `Speaker_Class.cpp` maps that to `mck_io_num`, but I don’t see us overriding `pin_mck` anywhere. So if you’re seeing `mck_io_num`, that’s coming from M5Unified’s internal/default speaker config, not from our `AudioManager` speaker setup.

Inside `.pio/libdeps`, M5Unified does assign `pin_mck` for some board profiles, and `Speaker_Class.cpp` maps that to `mck_io_num`, but I don’t see us overriding `pin_mck` anywhere. user_prompt: make it configurable in `inc\pins.h`, but leave it unused still, later with the external codec we will need to choose a pin
agent_reply: Done. I added `kNS4168SpeakerMclk = -1` in [inc/pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:12), and changed the speaker I2S setup in [AudioManager.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:819) to use that constant.
Because it is `-1`, the behavior stays the same: MCLK is still unused. Later, when the external codec needs it, we can pick a real GPIO in `pins.h`.
One note: the build also left `src/version.c` modified, which looks like the usual generated build/version churn.
